We recommended and Management agreed that the City Accountant:
a) contact the accounting system provider with regard to the issues of the
system specifically on the recognition of PPE cost to be recorded and
computation of salvage value and depreciation, so that immediate action
shall be provided to correct the system’s problem;
b) regularly review and monitor the system-generated depreciation
schedule for the PPE and evaluate the correctness of data using the
underlying provisions and basis of computation based on the applicable
laws, rules and regulations;
c) recompute the depreciation of other items per PPE Accounts and
compare it to the system-generated amount and adjust discrepancies
accordingly for proper presentation in the Financial Statements; and
d) prepare the necessary adjusting entries to correct the understatement of
accumulated depreciation of the sampled items for each Property, Plant,
and Equipment (PPE) account. The following correcting entries are

c. Completed projects of CGK in the amount of ₱ 83,326,340.98 are still carried in
the books of accounts as Construction in Progress (CIP).
We recommended and Management agreed that the City Accountant require the
submission of Certificate of Completion from the City Engineering Office as basis
for its subsequent adjusting entry.
d. Ten (10) unserviceable assets valued at ₱1,160,814.00, based on Report on the
Physical Count of Property, Plant, and Equipment (RPCPPE), are still
recognized in the book of accounts.
We recommended and the management agreed that:
a) the City Accountant take up necessary adjusting and/or correcting
entries upon the receipt of disposal reports from GSO to derecognize
the unserviceable assets; and
b) the General Service Officer to classify assets that are temporarily idle,
under repair, or expected to return to use with a more accurate
description—such as "serviceable: under repair"—instead of labeling
them as "unserviceable".

We recommended and the management agreed that:
a) the City Accountant
i. communicate and confirm long outstanding accounts to the source
agency and identify if remaining funds from inter-agency fund
transfers are to be reverted to the source agency, to the bureau of
treasury or be retained by the city government;
ii. after confirmation, adjust the payable accounts if proven to be
undocumented and without existing claimants especially those
accounts which are outstanding for more than two (2) years as stated
in Section 98 of PD 1445; and
iii. discontinue recording of sale of accountable forms to Due to LGU
account and administer adjusting entry for proper recording as
follows:
Due to LGU 546,650.00
Government Equity 546,650.00
b) the City Treasurer
i. discontinue encoding the sale of AFs to the barangays to the system
under the Due to LGU account. Instead, Other Business Income
account title should be used for the sale of accountable forms; and
ii. ensure the proper recording of transactions in the system to avoid
misrepresentations.

We recommended and Management agreed on the following:

a. the General Services Office in coordination with the City Accounting
Office:
i. conduct quarterly reconciliation of records or as needed, to
minimize the number of PPE items needed to be reconciled at
year-end;
ii. observe the usage of a uniform property coding to ease the process
of reconciliation of records;
iii. ensure that specific details (eg. Engine number, chassis number,
plate number, serial number, others) of each property are recorded
to distinguish the said properties from another similar type of
property; and
iv. reconcile both records to reflect the accurate and complete balance
of PPE accounts.

b. the City Accountant:
i. prepare an adjusting entry to derecognize the Work/Zoo Animals
amounting to P300,000.00 due to loss/death of the biological assets;
and
ii. record project costs when incurred and not when paid to present
accurately the balances of asset and liability accounts.

c. the Local Chief Executive implement the conduct of one-time cleansing of
PPE accounts pursuant to COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31,
2020 in order to properly establish the existence, condition, and
accountability of all PPE accounts.

We recommended and Management agreed that:

a. the City Treasurer send written notice to the aforementioned payees for
them to be aware of their outstanding checks. These notices shall be sent to
the payees one month prior to the maturity of checks to become stale as
required by the Manual. Stale checks on the hand of the payees should be
returned and marked as “cancelled’ before any replacement;
b. if checks are returned by the payee, the City Accountant shall request a
certified copy of the DV from the Auditor’s Office for presentation to the
Local Chief Executive who shall countersign the check. The cancelled check
shall be reported and attached to the Reports of Checks Issued (RCI)
prepared at the period of cancellation;
c. if the payees ignore or did not respond to the notices sent by the City
Treasurer or if the payees are untraceable, the City Accountant shall prepare
the necessary adjusting entries as follows:

i. For stale checks over 6 months but not more than 2 years (in
compliance with Section 59 of NGAS Manual for LGU) –
Cash in Bank – LCCA P XXXXX
Accounts Payable P XXXXX

ii. For stale checks over 2 years (in compliance with Section 97 of PD
1445) –
Cash in Bank – LCCA P XXXXX
Prior period adjustment P XXXXX
d. the City Treasurer and City Accountant continuously monitor the status of
all the checks released in order to present fairly the balance of cash in bank
account in the financial statements.

We recommended and the Management agreed on the following:

a. the City DRRM Council with the assistance of the City DRRM Officer:

i. ensure that projects, programs, and activities to be implemented
from the LDRRM Fund should have a detailed schedule time
frame to avoid overlapping of activities and delays in the
implementation;
ii. evaluate the recurring unimplemented PPAs funded under the
LDRRM Fund as to whether or not this could still address current
issues and the most pressing problems encountered; and
iii. consider modifying the LDRRM plan and prioritize PPAs to be
funded under the LDRRM Fund based on their urgency and
impact on the community to avoid and minimize the recurrence of
unimplemented PPAs;
b. the City DRRM Officer:

i. send formal written notices to the implementing departments and
partner agencies detailing the PPAs that need to be implemented
to improve and maximize the utilization of the fund; and
ii. coordinate further with the project and program holders
responsible for the implementation of PPAs under the fund by
conducting follow-ups to monitor and track the progress of
implementations.
c. the Local Chief Executive:

i. require the project and program holders of the LDRRM fund to
fully implement those PPAs under their responsibility;
ii. consider implementing the projects funded “by Administration”
to improve efficiency on the completion of small projects; and
iii. see to it that all PPAs identified in the LDRRM Plan are fully
implemented to improve disaster preparedness capacity and
minimize potential risks related to unforeseen calamities and
disasters.

We recommended and the Management agreed that:

a. the City Development Council with the assistance of the City Planning and
Development Coordinator:

i. ensure that projects to be implemented from the 20% Development
Fund should be specific as to details such as, but not limited to project
location, schedule time frame of implementation and other necessary
details in order to avoid generic appropriations under the fund;
ii. coordinate with the project holders responsible for the implementation
of development projects in order to address issues and other setbacks
beforehand and consider thereof during planning stage in order to
minimize delays during implementation phase;
iii. evaluate the existing unimplemented projects funded under the 20%
DF in accordance with the JMC if there is a need to realign or re-
programs the projects that may be identified to be not priority or
could not address the current issues pertaining to socio-economic and
environmental concerns; and
iv. closely monitor the projects implemented including prior years’ projects
not yet fully implemented in order to avoid backlogs and minimize the
risk of price change.
b. the project holders, City Engineer and Head of the CENRO:

i. conduct pre-survey or inspections on the proposed sites for
development projects in order to identify possible hindrances
beforehand and inform the CPDO on the results thereof; and

ii. If there are existing facilities of electric cooperatives found on the
proposed site for development projects during pre-survey, inform
them in writing and copy furnished the DOE and NEA for the
relocation of the affected facilities, pursuant to DOE-DPHW Joint
Circular No. 2017-01 dated July 11, 2017.
c. the Local Chief Executive:

i. ensure that development projects funded under the 20% Development
Fund are itemized and specific instead of by lump-sum appropriation
or generic;
ii. if projects to be implemented are later to be identified during
implementation phase, make certain that before implementation,
these are identified through passing of resolution of the CDC and
shall be adopted by the Sangguniang Panglungsod for approval; and

iii. see to it that all projects identified as priority development projects
which are embodied in the Local Development Plan are fully
implemented to help achieve the desirable socio-economic
development and environmental outcomes of the City.

With the foregoing, we recommended, and the management agreed on the
following:
a) For the City General Service Officer to:
i. Collaborate with the City Accountant to investigate and reconcile the
discrepancies noted between the report on physical count of PPE and
the book balances.
ii. Maintain an updated property cards for each PPE in order to
adequately account the assets of the City.
iii. Properly categorize the properties into their correct account title in
RPCPPE in accordance with description provided in COA Circular No.
2015-009
b) For the City Accountant to:
i. Collaborate with the City GSO to develop a plan in order to reconcile
the noted discrepancies between the book balances and report on
physical count of PPE.
ii. Analyze reconciling items and draw journal entries to accurately reflect
the balances of the involved accounts.
iii. Continuously corroborate unaccounted PPE items using other
supporting documents in conjunction with the outcome of inventory
taking.
iv. Henceforth, coordinate with the Budget Office as far as practicable in
identification of proper accounts during budgeting, and ensure
recognition of PPE items under their respective appropriate account to
foster fairness in the reporting of individual account balances.

We recommended, and the management agreed on, the following:
a) For the City General Service Officer to:
i. Expedite the registration and transfer of title of land parcels in order to
assure the ownership and completely protect the interest of the City in
compliance with stated regulations and circulars;
ii. After registering the real properties in the LGU’s name, they shall
notify the Commission on Audit (COA) of the such registration as
stated in Article 47 of the Local Government Code.
b) For the Local Chief Executive to evaluate the need to augment manpower
capacity of the City General Services Office, and make necessary action
thereon.

Therefore, we recommended, and the management agreed on the following:
a) For the City General Service Officer to:
i. Prepare and submit the Report on the Physical Count of Local Road
Network;
ii. Maintain a complete Local Road Network Property Card for all roads
and its components; and
iii. Maintain a Local Road Inventory and Road Map.
b) For the City Accountant to:
i. Prepare and maintain Local Road Network Ledger Cards for the road
and its components namely: road lot, road pavement, drainage and
slope protection structures and other miscellaneous structure;
ii. Prepare a lapsing schedule for the computation of depreciation of each
road component; and
iii. Disclose the Road Network System in the Notes to Financial
Statements
c) For the City Engineer to:
i. Assist the City GSO in conducting the physical inventory on the Local
Road Network in order to come up with the Report on the Physical
Count of Local Road Network; and
ii. Provide City Accountant and the City GSO complete description and
cost segregation of road components of every road project undertaken
for them to have a basis in recording the road networks in the Local
Road Network Ledger Card and Local Road Network Property Card,
respectively.
d) For the City Assessor to help in determining the costing of the land where the
local road networks were erected.

a) Henceforth, ensure that proof of deliveries of PPE items or other Assets procured
under similar loan agreements or Letter of Credit, together with the
corresponding Inspection and Acceptance Report, are furnished to the CAO,
more especially those with no payments made yet approaching the end of every
Calendar Year, in order to facilitate recognition thereof in the books; and
b) For the Sanggunian Panlungsod, as assisted by the Local Finance Committee, to
hereafter ensure that amidst the adoption of more economical fiscal
administration, ensure that all applicable laws, rules and regulations are
observed, particularly in enacting supplemental budgets.

agreed on the following:

not fall under any of the specific payable accounts and instruct the assigned
personnel to prepare the adjusting entries to accurately reflect the correct
balances of the aforementioned misclassified accounts

c) Exhaust all legal means to prove that the unverified P2M payable item has indeed
without existing valid claimant, such as through formal correspondence with the
LBP, to determine proper treatment thereof in accordance with Section 98 of PD
1445.

Therefore, we recommended, and the management agreed on the following:
a) For the City Accountant:
i. to reconcile the balances of the CIP accounts, more particularly those
attributable to 20% LDF and 5% DRRMF, with the pertinent records of
Engineering and other offices, to determine the status of remaining
balances, and immediately transfer all completed PPAs to proper PPE
accounts and accordingly record depreciation therefor. Likewise,
reconcile pertinent balances with the year-end report of the CPDO to
record ongoing PPAs for which no billings yet were forwarded to the
Accounting Office;
ii. to facilitate the preparation and submission of the remaining twenty-
three (23) CIP ledgers, pertaining to DRRM infrastructure projects; and
iii. to evaluate the need and viability to enhance the accounting system so as
to enable separate special accounts and financial reporting specifically
for DRRMF, and thereafter, make necessary actions to facilitate better
monitoring of and reporting on DRRMF.
b) For the City Engineering Office, to provide periodically the Accounting
Office of the status of projects and the procurements undertaken by the City,
to assist in the periodic review and monitoring of the balances of the PPAs.
The status report must be supported with Certificate of Completion on
completed PPAs and Statement of Work Accomplished on on-going PPAs.

Therefore, based on the foregoing audit observations, we recommended, and
the City Accountant agreed on, the following:
a) Strictly comply with the pertinent provisions of P.D. No. 1445 and COA Circular
No. 97-002 on the grant, utilization, and liquidation of cash advances and enforce
all possible remedies to settle outstanding cash advances;
b) Discontinue the practice of granting cash advances without ascertaining its
purpose, and consequently, ensure that corresponding liquidations are made in
accordance with COA Circular 97-002;
c) Establish a more stringent internal control over the granting and liquidation of
cash advances to employees from attached agencies, particularly those who are
not required to secure the LGU’s clearance from accountability, prior to their
transfer or separation from the LGU;
d) Exhaust all legal remedies as advised by the LGU legal office, to collect and/or
enforce liquidation from the persons liable, especially those who are no longer
associated or working in the LGU, including persons under AWOL;
e) Enforce the COA Order of Execution (COE) by requiring the withholding of
salaries or other compensation due to the person whose disallowances/charges
remained unsettled, in satisfaction of the latter, in accordance with COA Circular
No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009;
f) Properly reflect in the Financial Statement the correct balances of receivables-
disallowances/charges as well as loan receivable-others accounts;
g) Evaluate viability of adopting the provision of COA Circular 2016-005, which
prescribes the guidelines and procedures in reconciling and cleansing the
accounting books from non-performing receivable accounts; and
h) Coordinate with the local finance committee and other concerned offices to
ascertain the status of collectability of loans granted under livelihood project,
considering the material amount involved, and thereafter, carry out necessary
action either to facilitate collection therefor or otherwise inclusion in the items
for write-off in accordance with the immediately preceding audit
recommendation.

With the foregoing, we recommended, and the City Accountant agreed on,
the following:
a) substantiate, reconcile, and record necessary adjusting entries for valid book
reconciling items as reflected in the BRS, such as unrecognized bank debits and
credits, long existing unreconciled items, and book errors, among others;
b) henceforth, request copies of debit and credit memos from the bank for proper
and prompt recording thereof in the books of accounts; and
c) coordinate with the bank personnel relative to the identified bank errors and
make a representation to effect the necessary adjustments.

Therefore, we recommended, and the management agreed on, the following:
a) For the Local Chief Executive, as the Chairman of the Local School Board, to
ensure that concerned officials’ review of Programs, Projects and Activities
(PPAs) proposed under SEF were intensified, and that pertinent provisions of
Section 272 of R.A. No. 7160, Item 4.0 of DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No.
01 series of 2017 and other applicable guidelines in the utilization of SEF, were all
observed accordingly.
b) For the City Local School Board, to henceforth prioritize only those PPAs that
are authorized by the foregoing provisions, in the preparation of the Annual
School Board Budget for the SEF.

With the foregoing, we recommended and the management agreed on, the
following:
a) For the City Budget and DRRM Officers, as head of their respective
departments, to ensure henceforth that the designated budget monitoring
personnel over DRRMF intensify monitoring measures thereon.
b) For the City Accountant, hereafter, to ensure that the concerned accounting
personnel process only requests for disbursement against DRRMF, which have
appropriately passed the budget monitoring review by the CBO and CDRRMO.

With the foregoing, we recommended and the management agreed on, the
following:
a) secure adequate fidelity bond for all its accountable officers in order to
safeguard the assets in case of loss through theft, misappropriation and other
loss due to fortuitous events;
b) henceforth, monitor regularly the changes in the amounts of accountability of all
accountable officers and ensure accordingly that sufficient bond coverages are
secured; and
c) ensure adequacy in yearly budget for necessary fidelity bonds premiums.

Therefore, we recommended, and the management agreed on the following:
a) For the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) Chairman to:
i. instruct the BAC Secretariat to ensure that the NOAs and NTPs are
henceforth issued to the supplier/contractor within allowable period in
accordance with the related provision of the IRR of RA No. 9184;
ii. make sure that the allowable period of posting of NOAs and NTPs to
PhilGEPs website are henceforth followed through an effective
monitoring system;
iii. direct the BAC Secretariat and staff to issue notice to losing bidder and
attach the same together with other supporting documents to the DVs;
and
iv. evaluate the need to augment the manpower capacity of the BAC
Secretariat, and make necessary action as a result thereof to facilitate
preparation and submission of all pertinent reports and documents.
b) We also recommend the City Accountant to intensify the review of the
completeness of supporting documents more particularly those pertaining to
DVs on procurement, such as, but not limited to the receipted copy of the
notice to losing bidder.

With the foregoing, we recommend the following:
a) For the City Accountant;
i. to ensure that during reconciliation of actual fund utilization with
CDRRMO, those that pertains to the related Continuing Appropriations
as well as CIP of Capital Outlay projects, are also considered;
ii. to conduct regular reconciliation of actual project costs with the
pertinent records and reports of the CEO, with the view of establishing
the correct CIP balances, which will eventually be recorded in the
appropriate PPE accounts; and
iii. verify the veracity of all accounting data with discrepancies, as indicated
in Tables 25-27, and after proper reconciliation procedures, effect
necessary adjusting journal entries.
b) For the City Engineer, to provide regularly the CDRRMO, with pertinent
reports containing update on the actual status of implementation of DRRM
infrastructure projects as well as the actual costing thereof.
c) For the CDRRM Officer, as the main program holder of DRRM-PPAs, to
ensure that relevant reports of the department, more particularly concerning
the actual status of completion and actual costing of DRRM infrastructure
projects, are verified against related data from the CEO, in order to have a
more reliable data furnished to the CAO.

Therefore, in view of the foregoing observations, we recommended and the
management agreed on the following:
a) The Local Chief Executive, as the chairman of the LGU’s Committee on Program
on Awards and Incentives for Service Excellence (PRAISE), to:
i. henceforth ensure that all awards and incentives to be granted by the LGU
are in accordance with the (PRAISE) as approved by the Civil Service
Commission (CSC), and all applicable laws, rules and regulations; and
ii. ensure that the PRAISE Committee and related TWG will steadfastly carry-
out its responsibilities and facilitate compliance with all requirements set
forth in CSC Memorandum Circular No. 01, Series of 2001.
b) The Sangguniang Panlungsod to make certain that no annual or supplemental
budgets are enacted to fund the grant of awards and incentives to LGU
employees and officers, unless in accordance with the CSC-approved PRAISE,
and all applicable laws, rules and regulations.

To address the above observations, we recommended to the City
Accountant, with the City HRM Officer, to which they agreed, the following:
a) Coordinate accordingly with the HDMF and GSIS offices with regard to the
discrepancies in the remittances of loan amortizations, to settle the gaps
noted. Thereafter, reconcile and update the agency records, if necessary.
b) Henceforth, remit promptly all required loan amortizations of LGU officials
and employees to HDMF and GSIS to avoid incurrence of penalty/surcharges
and enable member-employees to continuously avail of benefits due them.
c) Ensure prioritization of HDMF and GSIS obligations together with other
mandatory obligations in salary deductions of all its officers and employees.
d) Hereafter, strictly ascertain the adequacy of the capacity to pay of employee-
members, with reference to the mandated minimum net-take-home-pay set
by DBM, before endorsing their loan applications to HDMF and GSIS.

Therefore, we recommended and the management agreed on the following:
a) The Sangguniang Panlungsod to expedite evaluation of the proposals to
increase the number of personnel plantilla of the City Government,
especially that which pertains to the City Planning and Development
Office.
b) The Local Chief Executive to:
i. in coordination with the City Development Council (CDC) and the Local
Finance Committee (LFC), and in consideration of the limited manpower
available to implement 20% DF PPAs promptly, consider in the planning
and budgeting phases prioritization of relevant and feasible PPAs,
strategically develop mechanisms to optimize utilization of the fund and
realization of socio-economic development and environmental
management outcomes; and
ii. require concerned implementing departments to be committed in
carrying-out the PPAs as planned.c) The City Planning Development Coordinator to:
i. conduct a thorough planning for the implementation of development
projects and consider the appropriate timing and phasing of their
implementation;
ii. coordinate with the concerned offices with regard to PPAs that will be
included in the 20% DF AIP to determine the specific works to be done
thereon and to avoid delays in the implementation; and
iii. strategize monitoring of all PPAs and preparation of pertinent reports by
optimizing available manpower capacity.d) The City Engineer continue the resort of hiring JOs per project type basis to
facilitate timely preparation of technical documents, pending the approval of
the request to increase the number of personnel plantilla.

With the foregoing, we recommended and the CDRRMO agreed on the
following:
a) As the secretariat of the City DRRM Council, to steadfastly monitor the status of
implementation of DRRM PPAs purposely to identify promptly, both PPAs
which eventually become not implementable, and savings from budget allotments
not fully utilizable, to enable recommending, accordingly, to the City DRRM
Council the re-programming of unused funds to other priority DRRM-related
PPAs.
b) In coordination with the concerned departments, to re-evaluate viability of
existing options available to the LGU pertaining to the planned procurement of
various lands, and thereafter reassess the need to look for other options to
facilitate procurement therefor, thereby hasten the realization of the respective
purposes of intended land acquisition.

Therefore, we recommended and the CDRRMO agreed on the
following:
a) In coordination with the CAO, CBO and CEO, to steadfastly monitor the
status of completion of DRRM-PPAs, particularly those involving capital
outlay or infrastructure projects funded by current year 5% DRRMF as
well as by Continuing Appropriation, with the view of identifying savings
from completed projects.
b) Recommend promptly and accordingly to the CDRRMC and CBO the
identified savings from completed DRRM infrastructure projects, to aid
them on their evaluation of available funding sources during budget
preparation for LGU priority PPAs.

Therefore, we recommended the following;
For the City General Service Officer to:
a. Develop, formulate, and implement strategies, policies and procedures in the conduct of Physical Inventory of PPEs. 
b. Together with the City Accountant, investigate and clear all discrepancies between physical and book inventories. If necessary, require written explanation from the persons responsible. 
c. Coordinate with the City Engineering Office particularly on public infrastructure accounts which constitute large portion of variance to monitor the proper amount to be recorded in the CGSO Report
d. Provide property number on all PPEs reported for easy identification of the properties
e. Use the prescribed format of the Inventory Report prescribed in Government Accounting Manual for LGUs.

• Physical Inventory Plan (PIP) has been prepared and implemented.
• CEO submitted to the CGSO on public completed infra projects

observations.recommendation:
Therefore, we recommended that the City Accountant make the necessary adjusting entries to reflect the correct account type of the bank accounts and provide proper audit trail by following the suggested journal entry:
[No journal entry shown in table excerpt.]
Moreover, we recommended for the City Treasurer, in coordination with the other officials, to enroll the accounts of the City on the available online platforms of the AGDBs (e.g., We Access of LBP) to automatically check transaction history of the accounts which could be used to prepare the BRS.
observations.managementAction:
Fully implemented
• Adjusting entries were prepared and posted; accounts reclassified accordingly. 
• City Treasurer to enroll in online platform was implemented.

observations.recommendation:
Henceforth, we recommended and the City Accountant agreed on the following:

observations.recommendation:
For the City General Service Officer;

a. Develop, formulate, and implement strategies, policies and procedures
in the conduct of Physical Inventory of PPEs and reconciliation of
balances with the City Accounting Office;
b. Together with the City Accountant, investigate and clear all
discrepancies between physical and book inventories. If necessary,
require written explanation from the persons responsible.
c. Coordinate with the City Engineering Office particularly on public
infrastructure accounts which constitute large portion of variance to
monitor the proper amount to be recorded in the CGSO Report
d. Provide property number on all the PPEs reported for easy
identification of the properties
e. Use the prescribed format of the Inventory Report prescribed in
Government Accounting Manual for LGUs.

For the City Accountant to;
a. Devise a plan together with the CGSO to reconcile the difference
between the noted PPE items on books and the Inventory Report. If
necessary, require written explanation from the persons responsible on
the deficiencies noted.
b. Analyze the reconciling items and prepare journal entries to reflect the
correct balances of the affected accounts
c. Continue reconstructing the PPEs with noted “for reconciliation” so
that proper amounts will be presented in the Financial Statement.

• Make necessary effort to provide information of the PPEs not
provided with depreciation by coordinating with other
Offices/Departments and provide the depreciation if complete data
has been gathered;
• Check the accuracy of information entered in the accounting system
and make sure all data of the PPEs are provided;
• Recompute the computation of depreciation once entered into the
system to verify and establish the correct amount;
• Adjust the understatement of Accumulated Depreciation in
previous years to be able to present the PPE at its proper valuation.
• Coordinate with the system provider of the accounting system used
by the Accounting Office to correct the computation of the
provision for depreciation

• Conduct disposal of the remaining unserviceable properties in
accordance with the guidelines set in the HSPMS. If budget permits,
provide additional staff to give attention on the inventory and
disposal of unserviceable properties
• Submit the necessary documents to the Office of the City
Accountant, after the disposal, as basis of the latter in dropping the
PPEs in the books of accounts
• Continue reconciling with the record of the City Accountant with
regard to the already disposed PPEs for proper dropping in the
books.

We also recommended that the City Accountant, after the disposal, to
draw journal entry vouchers to derecognize the properties and apply
appropriate accounting procedures to effect adjustments in the books.
Also, make extra effort in reconciling PPEs already disposed but were not
yet dropped in the books of accounts.

observations.recommendation:
Therefore, to have a more reliable financial reporting and effective property management of the agency over the assets. We recommended the following:
1. Require the City Treasurer to:
• Establish proper controls over disbursements of funds especially to those funds which have common bank account which is not in consonance to sound internal control procedures
2. Refrain from disbursing funds without cash back up thus funds were not monitored / controlled, disrupting the operations of the affected account
3. Inform concerned chief of office handling the accounts on the insufficiency of balances on the required information as to disbursements so that the proper subsidy of funds will be recorded and accounted
4. Ensure that disbursements are charged against the appropriate bank accounts and collections are deposited in the correct bank accounts
observations.reason: Implemented

observations.recommendation:
Therefore, we recommended that the City Accountant will do the following to present fairly the receivable accounts of the City in the Financial Statements:

a. Conduct regular monitoring and analysis of receivable accounts to ensure that these are collected when these become demandable and that cash advances are liquidated within the prescribed period depending upon their nature and purpose
b. Exert effort to demand immediately the liquidation of all outstanding cash advances from the concerned officials/employees who are still connected with the City and consider taking legal actions/remedy against them.
c. Impose sanctions prescribed in item 5.1.3 of COA Circular No. 1997-0021 for non-compliance of liquidating cash advances, such as withholding the salaries and other emoluments of the officials/employees who continuously disregard, fail or decline to comply with the demand, including filing appropriate charges against them
d. Send demand letters to officials and employees who are no longer connected with the City but whose whereabouts are still known to liquidate their cash advances/receivables and consider taking legal actions/remedy against them.
e. Gather all documents that will prove that liquidation/payment of the named individuals is no longer possible such as those who are already deceased (e.g. death certificate) and whose last address is not known (e.g. retirement certification, clearances, etc.) and request for the write off of the receivable for fair presentation of the account in the Financial Statements taking into consideration the liability of those individuals who granted the clearances. Exclude for request for write-off individuals whose receivable arise from disallowances and charges, cash shortages and subject of a pending case in court or before investigative authorities.
f. Prepare the list of those who have made payments on the unrecorded COLA and retrieve supporting documents thereto and present the same to COA for proper issuance of settlement. Subsequently, make the necessary adjustments in the books
g.Make a report on the negative balances of accounts of accountable officer and payback what has been over collected, if applicable upon conduct of review on the accounts.

Also, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive assign an individual or group of individuals to develop an internal control procedure to ensure proper monitoring of the loan payments granted to organized groups or individuals and guarantee the collectability of the same.

d. Review and investigate the negative balances and make the necessary demand on the payables of the management against the concerned supplier/creditor/employee, if applicable; and
e. Make the necessary adjustments in the books should review and investigation of the negative balances were due to erroneous recording.

Management’s comment:
The management assured the audit team that extensive efforts would be employed
to reconcile all cash accounts with the bank balances and to adjust the reconciling items
after the proper verification of related documents. Also, bank reconciliations will be
prepared for all bank accounts of the agency and evaluate the non-moving accounts to
determine whether closure of bank accounts shall be made.

a. Locate and trace down the documents to support the adjusting entries
on the long outstanding reconciling items
b. Coordinate, reconcile and investigate the book errors;
c. Request copies from the bank of the debit memos and credit memos
issued for proper recording in the books of accounts;
d. Record adjustments for valid reconciling items per books as reflected in
the BRS prepared;
e. Coordinate with the bank personnel relative to the identified bank errors
and make a representation to make the necessary adjustments;
f. Locate the stale checks if it is still with the City Treasurer and make the
necessary adjustments. If the check is not available, confirm the payable
account with the payee and make the proper procedures for replacing
the check. If no response was received, make the necessary adjustments
with the books.

 Establish proper controls over disbursements of funds especially to those
funds which have common bank account
 Refrain from disbursing funds without cash back up
 Inform concerned chief of office handling the accounts on the
insufficiency of balances on required disbursements so that proper subsidy
of funds will be recorded and accounted
 Ensure that disbursements are charged against the appropriate bank
accounts and collections are deposited in the correct bank accounts

a. Conduct regular monitoring and analysis of receivable accounts to
ensure that these are collected when these become due and
demandable and that cash advances are liquidated within the
prescribed period depending upon their nature and purpose
b. Exert effort to demand immediately the liquidation of all outstanding
cash advances from the concerned officials/employees who are still
connected with the City
c. Impose sanctions prescribed in item 5.1.3 of COA Circular No. 1997-
0024 for non-compliance of liquidating cash advances, such as
withholding the salaries and other emoluments of the
officials/employees who continuously disregard, fail or decline to
comply with the demand, including filing appropriate charges against
them
d. Send demand letters to officials and employees who are no longer
connected with the City but whose whereabouts are still known to
liquidate their cash advances/ receivables and consider taking legal
actions/remedy against them.
e. Gather all documents that will prove that liquidation/payment of the
named individuals is no longer possible such as those who are already
deceased (e.g. death certificate) and whose last address is not known
(e.g. retirement certification, clearances, etc.) and request for the write
off of the receivable for fair presentation of the account in the
Financial Statements taking into consideration the liability of those
individuals who granted the clearances. Exclude for request for write-
off individuals whose receivable arise from disallowances and
charges, cash shortages and subject of a pending case in court or
before investigative authorities.
f. Prepare the list of those who have made payments on the unrecorded
COLA and retrieve supporting documents thereto and present the
same to COA for proper issuance of settlement. Subsequently, make
the necessary adjustments in the books
g. Make a report on the negative balances of accounts of accountable
officer and payback what has been over collected, if applicable upon
conduct of review on the accounts.

5.11. Also, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive assign an individual or
group of individuals to develop an internal control procedure to ensure proper
monitoring of the loan payments granted to organized groups or individuals and
guarantee the collectability of the same.

a. Confirm the amount of the payables from the list of the identified creditors

c. Review the validity of accounts payable in a regular basis to promptly
determine which of the accounts are already dormant and to take necessary

demand on the receivable of the management against the concerned
supplier/creditor/employee, if applicable; and
e. Make the necessary adjustments in the books should review and
investigation of the negative balances were due to erroneous recording.

Therefore, we recommended that the City Planning and Development Office to
ensure the inclusion of all the on-going as well as all projects that are to be
implemented and are yet to be implemented for the reporting year in
consideration with the actual amounts incurred to warrant transparency and
provide auditable information that could be verified by all stakeholders of the
LGU for sound decision-making.

the Accounting Office.

CDRRMO shall submit the report on or before the 15th day after the end of each
month through the LDRRMC and Local Development Council (LDC) to the

For the City Planning and Development Coordinator to:
 Closely monitor the utilization and implementation of priority projects for
the attainment of desirable socio-economic development and environmental
management outcomes and to maximize the usage of the fund;
 Recommend to the Local Development Council to reprogram savings out
of the completed projects (both from continuing and current appropriation)
 Evaluate projects before presentation to the Local Development Council to
minimize number of abandoned projects.
For the Concerned Implementing Office on proposed PPAs to:
 Coordinate with the City Engineering office and ensure that program of
works, project designs, coordination with other agencies and other needed
documents needing approval had been prepared and approved without
objections prior to its inclusion to the Annual Investment Plan
 Fast track the implementation based on the scheduled time frames indicated
in the AIP to achieve the desired management outcomes

Therefore, we recommended for the CDRRMO, in coordination with the Local
Disaster Risk Deduction Management Council (LDRRMC), to:
a. Ensure that only the PPAs culled from the Local Development Investment
Plan are included in the Annual Investment Plan to ensure that the
implemented PPAs contributed to the achievement of long-term goals in
managing hazard vulnerabilities and risks.
b. Revisit PPAs in the LDIP if such were still feasible to address current
development in the City and revise the same, if need be, in accordance to
the long-term objectives of the LGU
c. Conduct thorough situational analysis (research and development) of the
City to understand risk information which includes national hazards,
vulnerabilities and climate change risks for development to alleviate
poverty and environmental protection consistent with the purpose of the
fund to identify immediately needed activities to prioritize PPAs
incorporated in the LDIP; and
d. Harmonize local planning, investment programming, revenue
administration, budgeting, and expenditure management guidelines
prescribed in the DBM-NEDA-DILG-DOF Joint Memorandum Circular
No. 1, series of 2007 dated March 8, 2007.

a. Allocate enough funds from the General Fund (GF) to subsidize the
maintenance and other operating expenses (MOOE) of the CDRRM Office,
in order not to lessen the fund intended for disaster related activities.
b. Revisit the CDRRM Plan and make the necessary adjustments to ensure
alignment of PPAs with JMC No. 2014-1 of the NDRRMC, DILG, DBM
and CSC especially on the administrative expenses and honorarium aspect
c. Henceforth, stop granting honorarium to volunteers/responders in the
delivery of disaster risk reduction programs and activities.

 Closely monitor the undertaking of proposed projects funded out of
LDRRMF to avoid/minimize disaster projected and unexpected risks, and
enhance disaster preparedness and response capabilities at all levels.
 Utilize the unexpended portion of the Trust Fund of DRRMF and prepare
a comprehensive CDRRMF Plan to include both the current appropriations
and unexpended balance in the Special Trust Fund.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed on the following:
1. Require the City Accountant to facilitate the preparation of Bank Reconciliation
Statements for all bank accounts in conformity with existing guidelines and submit
the same to the Office of the Auditor on the required period.
2. Locate and investigate the discrepancies noted and effect the corrections by making
necessary adjustments to the affected accounts.
3. Use existing LBP account solely for collections and disbursements of Local
Economic Enterprises – Public Market, Slaughterhouse and Public Cemetery.
4. Maintain DBP account for the collections and disbursements of the 20% Local
Development Fund and GF-Proper with the corresponding subsidiary ledger.
5. Cease from using two different bank accounts for deposits and disbursement,
instead, maintain only one account for deposits and disbursements.
6. Revisit and comply strictly with the provisions of COA Circular No. 96-011 dated
October 2, 1996.

We recommended and Management agreed on the following:
1. Require the City Accountant and the General Service Officer to conduct periodic
reconciliation on their respective records in order to have a fair presentation of the
Property, Plant and Equipment account in the financial statements. Determine the
status of PPE in order to properly account those that are serviceable and non-
serviceable assets;
2. Observe a uniform property coding to ease the process of reconciliation; and
3. Strictly comply with the provisions of Section 114 of NGAS Manual for LGU
Volume I.

We recommended and Management agreed on the following:
1. Allocate 1% of IRA and include the same in the annual budget of the local
government unit for the strengthening and implementation of the Juvenile
Intervention Program in accordance with existing law; and
2. Require the City Councils for the Protection of Children to develop a three-year
comprehensive Juvenile Intervention Program to address the needs of the children
in the locality, particularly on delinquency prevention.

We recommended and Management agreed on the following:
1. Require the City School Board to evaluate and carefully select PPAs to be funded
out of the SEF in accordance with the provisions of DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint
Circular No. 1, series 2017
2. Coordinate with the Public Schools District Supervisor (PSDS) and School Division
Superintendent (SDS) with regard to their recommendations as well as priority
PPAs to be included in the Annual SEF budget.
3. Stop charging expenditures not related to the purpose of the SEF to avoid future
disallowance.
4. Strictly comply with the provisions under Sections 100 and 272 of RA 7160.

We recommended and Management agreed on the following:
1. Instruct the CEED Officer to evaluate the existing LEEs using the manual15
provided by the DBM in the setting up and operations of LEEs and make
appropriate actions based on the evaluation whether to continue or discontinue
the LEEs;
2. Revisit Local Budget Circular No. 111 for a more effective management of local
economic enterprises; and
3. Revisit and strictly comply with the provisions of Local Budget Circular No. 100
dated October 1, 2012, particularly on Section 3.3 of the same.

observations.recommendation:
a) Require the Franchising Section to submit the complete reconciled list of codified Motorized Tricycle Operators who availed of the Tricycle painting to the Accounting Office for proper recording and adjustments. We suggest the following adjusting entry.
b) Reconciliation of records must be performed by the Franchising Section with Management Information System (MIS) and the Accounting Office every month to come up with the correct balance.
c) In subsequent granting of loans to tricycle Operators, if any, a promissory note or a Memorandum of Agreement (MOA) must be prepared to evidence the availment and receipt of the loan.
d) The Franchising Section together with the Accounting Office must device a scheme on how to collect payment of loan. Collections will be used for operations of the City of Koronadal. Exert more effort to collect from the Tricycle Operators who had availed of the loan of P1, 500.00. Demand letters to Tricycle Operators must be issued specially to those with cancelled Motorized Tricycle Operator’s Permit (MTOP).
e) The committee on Transportation and the Franchising Section should further study or device a scheme that would address the proliferation of illegal tricycles plying within the territorial jurisdiction of the City of Koronadal in consonance to Sangguniang Panlungsod (SP) Resolution No. 1087, series of 2012 and Ordinance No. 08, series of 2012. It is recommended to issue new MTOPs in lieu of cancelled MTOPs to minimize or address illegal tricycles. In this manner, additional income is also realized in accordance to City Ordinance No. 01, series of 2008.

Recommendations:
We recommended the following:
1. Refund from the GF to SEF the amount of P1,842,327.17, representing the
P872,974.78 as payment of personal services to various casual City
employees and the P969,352.36 disbursements for various activities and
expenses of various DepEd officials and public school teachers;
2. Discontinue the practice of charging against the SEF, the payment of salaries,
allowances and other benefits of clerks, administrative aides, laborer and
security guards who are employed as casual employees of the City,
regardless of their place of assignments to various public schools. Such
expenses are chargeable to the general fund of the City subject to existing
auditing rules and regulations; and
3. Henceforth, restrict the allocation and prioritization of the SEF to the
programs and projects specifically defined under the afore-stated law, rules
and regulations in order to maximize the benefits that the public elementary
and secondary schools could derive from this fund.

We recommended the following:
1. Management should conduct regular cleaning and maintenance of the area
surrounding the BHS and comply with the standard requirements on
infrastructure, conditions and amenities, pursuant to DOH guidelines and The
Ecological Solid Waste Management Act of 2000;
2. Management should keep complete and updated inventory stock cards and
supply records, to be able to ascertain the need of patients prior to the conduct
of medical programs. Enumerated items previously identified as unavailable
should be immediately requested from the city health office.
3. Management should ensure that basic training program is provided to all
BHWs, to ensure that health services being offered are in accordance with
existing program guidelines.

We recommended the following:
1. Management should ensure that BHWs are accordingly compensated and
given incentives in recognition of their services;
2. Further, management should confirm that the BHWs are duly registered with
the local health board in the city, in order to entitle their BHWs to benefits and
incentives provided under existing laws.
In addition, maintain records of each financial transactions to avoid having accounts with no details.
